body.text: I knew there was no end to our deployment cycle of being gone for half the year, 90 days a time.
And I knew my husband wasn't responsible or trustworthy enough to be left alone with a baby.
I knew what I had to do. And I did.
I left my could-have-been high-flying career as an Air Force Officer.
But my kid was so worth it. She was — and is — amazing.
I knew if I only had one, she was perfect.
Another move across country to be near family. Surely, having love and support would make this easier, right?
Then came another episode: Alcohol. Rage. Abuse.
Glass objects were chucked on the floor where my daughter played and crawled.
I was thrown up against a door jamb, my back bruised.
That was it. I was done.
It honestly wasn't for me. It was for my kid. I just couldn't risk it any more.
I threw him out. He didn't come back — for the most part.
